pages.title: Who am I – Luke Myer MP for Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland
pages.meta_description: I was born and raised here; this is my home. I grew up in Brotton in East Cleveland - my dad worked for a foodbank, and my mam worked in a local library. He proposed to her over the tannoy at Ayresome Park; I’m proud to be Teesside to the core. I believe our home…
headings: h2: I was born and raised here; this is my home.
pages.text_excerpt: Who am I – Luke Myer MP for Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland Who I am What I do How I can help Surgeries News South Middlesbrough Guisborough East Cleveland I was born and raised here; this is my home. I grew up in Brotton in East Cleveland – my dad worked for a foodbank, and my mam worked in a local library. He proposed to her over the tannoy at Ayresome Park; I’m proud to be Teesside to the core . I believe our home is the best place in the world – full of brilliant people and proud heritage. It’s where I married my wife, Beth, and where I still live today. I love our communities , and that’s why I first got into politics: to serve our area as a Labour councillor. I won my seat from the Tories in one of their strongest parts of East Cleveland, and led the council’s children’s services department, delivering action on child poverty and better help for families. But I saw firsthand how our area was getting battered by fourteen years of a Tory government in London who didn’t understand the damage they were doing. That’s why I got on the pitch and ran to be our MP. It was a long shot: running against a former minister with a nearly 12,000 majority. But I grafted hard, brought people together, and set out a bold plan for change – and we won. Now, I’m working to bring change for our people. Teesside built the world, and just like our steel, our communities were forged under tough pressure too. I’m working to restore that sense of common purpose , give our young people the best start in life, and get our economy moving again with the big ideas of the future. A new generation of leadership, and a different style of politics. With your support, we can deliver that change. Thank you. What I do Facebook Instagram Link YouTube Mail Privacy Policy Behaviour Policy Promoted by Luke Myer, 28B Chaloner St, Guisborough, TS14 6QD

pages.title: Industry leaders convened in Parliament for Carbon Capture push – Luke Myer MP for Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland
pages.meta_description: Today (29 April 2026) Luke Myer MP brought together industry leaders in Westminster to drive forward plans for cleaner growth and jobs for Teesside. Luke Myer MP chaired the roundtable in Parliament on Wednesday with leaders from across the rail freight, energy and industrial sectors to discuss the future of non-pipeline transport of CO₂ –…
headings: h2: Industry leaders convened in Parliament for Carbon Capture push
h2: Discover more from Luke Myer MP for Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland
pages.text_excerpt: Industry leaders convened in Parliament for Carbon Capture push – Luke Myer MP for Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland Who I am What I do How I can help Surgeries News South Middlesbrough Guisborough East Cleveland Business and Trade , Economy , Energy Industry leaders convened in Parliament for Carbon Capture push Today (29 April 2026) Luke Myer MP brought together industry leaders in Westminster to drive forward plans for cleaner growth and jobs for Teesside. Luke Myer MP chaired the roundtable in Parliament on Wednesday with leaders from across the rail freight, energy and industrial sectors to discuss the future of non-pipeline transport of CO₂ – a key part of delivering carbon capture, usage and storage (CCUS) in the UK. The carbon capture industry is rapidly scaling up on Teesside, following the £4bn Northern Endurance Partnership project being granted approval from the Labour Government in 2024. The meeting today brought together organisations including Navigator Terminals, the Rail Freight Group, trade associations, officials from the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero, NEPIC and major industrial leaders. Contributions from industry and government highlighted both the scale of the opportunity and the practical challenges that must be addressed to make non-pipeline transport viable at scale. The roundtable forms part of Luke’s ongoing work as a voice on steel, energy and industrial strategy, and his engagement with businesses across Teesside and the wider North East. Luke Myer MP said: “Teesside has always been at the forefront of Britain’s industry, and carbon capture is a huge opportunity for our future. Bringing together industry and government in Parliament is about turning that potential into reality – creating good jobs, backing our industries, and making sure places like ours lead the clean energy transition.” Share this: Share on X (Opens in new window) X Share on Facebook (Opens in new window) Facebook Like this: Like Loading… Previous Post Luke leads talks to tackle antisocial behaviour in Coulby Newham Next Post Families to save up to £1,000 as children’s reforms become law Facebook Instagram Link YouTube Mail Privacy Policy Behaviour Policy Promoted by Luke Myer, 28B Chaloner St, Guisborough, TS14 6QD Discover more from Luke Myer MP for Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland Subscribe now to keep reading and get access to the full archive. pages.meta_description: Offshore wind in the North East has been given a major boost today (27 April 2026), as new rules have been passed in Parliament to unlock investment and speed up clean energy projects. The changes overhaul how environmental protections are applied at sea. Instead of developers being limited to very narrow, site-by-site fixes, they will…
headings: h2: Sea change for Teesside clean power jobs
h2: Discover more from Luke Myer MP for Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland
pages.text_excerpt: Sea change for Teesside clean power jobs – Luke Myer MP for Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland Who I am What I do How I can help Surgeries News South Middlesbrough Guisborough East Cleveland Economy , Energy , News Sea change for Teesside clean power jobs Offshore wind in the North East has been given a major boost today (27 April 2026), as new rules have been passed in Parliament to unlock investment and speed up clean energy projects. The changes overhaul how environmental protections are applied at sea. Instead of developers being limited to very narrow, site-by-site fixes, they will now be able to deliver general environmental improvements across the wider marine area. That means projects can go ahead faster, while still protecting nature. Luke Myer, MP for Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland, served on the committee which passed the reforms and said they could help drive jobs and growth across Teesside. Luke Myer MP said: “This is about backing British energy, creating jobs here at home and bringing bills down in the long run by boosting our own supply. Teesside can and should lead the way.” “People on Teesside want good jobs, lower bills and a secure future. With common sense regulations in place, offshore wind can help deliver all three. This is about making sure Britain can become a clean energy superpower, and Teesside can lead the way.” Teesside is already a clean energy powerhouse, with around 9,000 jobs in the sector across the region. By speeding up consent approvals, the region can bring in even more investment and create more skilled jobs in turbine manufacture and related industries. pages.meta_description: Behaviour and Contact Policy I am proud to represent the people of Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland. My constituency service is supported by a small team of dedicated staff, who work hard to assist as many local residents as possible, as quickly and effectively as they can. I understand that at times of distress or…
pages.text_excerpt: Behaviour and Contact Policy – Luke Myer MP for Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland Who I am What I do How I can help Surgeries News South Middlesbrough Guisborough East Cleveland Behaviour and Contact Policy I am proud to represent the people of Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland. My constituency service is supported by a small team of dedicated staff, who work hard to assist as many local residents as possible, as quickly and effectively as they can. I understand that at times of distress or frustration, people may act out of character. Many of those who contact my office have been through upsetting circumstances. However, I must also ensure that my staff can carry out their work in a safe and respectful environment. For this reason, I have put in place a policy to manage unacceptable behaviour and unreasonable demands. Aggressive or Abusive Behaviour While I recognise that constituents may feel upset or angry, aggression towards my staff is never acceptable. Abuse – whether verbal, written or physical – will not be tolerated, including any behaviour or language that causes members of my team to feel offended, threatened, or intimidated. Shouting, swearing, discriminatory language, as well as making serious allegations of corruption or criminality without evidence is not acceptable. If any member of my team feels scared or threatened during an interaction they may bring the conversation to an end immediately and, if necessary, contact the police. Unreasonable Demands and Levels of Contact My team must be able to manage their casework load fairly across all constituents. Excessive demands – such as repeated urgent requests, insisting on particular staff, changing complaints repeatedly, or overwhelming the office with emails on unrelated issues – can prevent others from receiving the help they need and is not acceptable. Harassment My staff have the right to a safe workplace which is free from harassment. Harassment includes the recording and publishing conversations without consent, contacting staff through personal channels or publishing personal information online. Actions My Office May Take : Issue a formal warning Appoint a single point of contact Restrict communications to writing only Decline to investigate complaints pursued unacceptably Block phone numbers or email addresses Report matters to the police if necessary Where formal action is taken, a note may be placed on our records. Facebook Instagram Link YouTube Mail Privacy Policy Behaviour Policy Promoted by Luke Myer, 28B Chaloner St, Guisborough, TS14 6QD
